title: StackSize
description: Allows you to adjust max stack sizes with a Fabric mod.
body:
ㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤ
 [![Icon](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/b10b6b734f58eecdfe271bb2e14d0ec6aca06a39.png)](https://modrinth.com/mod/stacksize)ㅤ
 # ㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤStackSize
 [![Mod Loader](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/47b9d8692502ad76483ca42c9e67af270de1c05e.png)](https://fabricmc.net)
![https://img.shields.io/badge/environment-server%2c%20opt%20client-c65135?style=flat-square](https://img.shields.io/badge/environment-server%2c%20opt%20client-c65135?style=flat-square)
#  ❓ How To Use
**Simply change the values in the config, or add new item types**
![Config](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/89fe15618078151eef8ff05f52ac645f9867fefd.png)
  ㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤㅤ**After this, you shouldn't be able to go past the specified stack limits, this counts for picking up items, clicking in the inventory etc.**

_As of v1.2, you can also edit stack limits with the /stacksize command in-game, however, this requires op._
### (NOTE: going over 64 does technically work but it's extremely buggy unless using the mod client side too)
### 
if you have any issues, join the [discord](https://discord.gg/j2esYGGttP).
